description: Solve the Unit Commitment (UC) problem — minimizing total generation fuel cost by deciding which generators are ON/OFF across time periods under load-balance constraints — using QAOA, compared with classical brute-force search.

inputs.generators: 6 units (A-F), p_max 300-1000 MW, quadratic fuel cost F = a + b*P + c*P^2
inputs.qubit_encoding: x[i,t] = unit i ON at period t; qubit index = i*T + t

outputs.solution: ON/OFF schedule per generator per period

primary_metric_definition: |QAOA_cost - classical_optimal_cost| / classical_optimal_cost * 100; lower is better (0% = optimal)
quantum_method: QAOA (Quantum Approximate Optimization Algorithm) with cqlib StatevectorSimulator, COBYLA optimizer, 2 layers, 5 restarts
classical_baseline: Brute-force enumeration of all 2^n bitstrings (feasible for n<=8)
success_signals: QAOA finds feasible solutions (load balance satisfied) across all tested scales
QAOA optimality gap <= 5% on small-to-medium scales (4-6 qubits)
QAOA scales to 8 qubits while remaining feasible and near-optimal
